skill.name: opensource-guide-coach
skill.description: Use when a user wants guidance on starting, contributing to, growing, governing, funding, securing, or sustaining an open source project, or asks about contributor onboarding, community health, maintainer burnout, code of conduct, metrics, legal basics, or open source project adoption.
skill.summary: Get expert guidance on open source project management using official Open Source Guides
skill.author: xixu-me

security_audit.summary: This is a pure documentation skill with no executable code. All static analysis findings are false positives caused by markdown syntax (backtick code spans) being misidentified as Ruby shell execution, and legitimate URLs to official Open Source Guides being flagged as hardcoded URLs. The license identifier CC-BY-4.0 was incorrectly flagged as weak cryptography. No security risks present.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backtick Syntax Misidentified
low_findings.description: Static analyzer detected 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' patterns, but these are markdown inline code spans (e.g., `starting-a-project`) used in documentation tables. This is a pure documentation skill with no executable code.

content.user_title: Navigate Open Source with Expert Coaching
content.value_statement: Get personalized guidance for open source projects using official Open Source Guides. Transform best practices into actionable plans for maintainers, contributors, and community managers.
actual_capabilities: Route open source questions to relevant official guides from opensource.guide
Provide tailored advice based on user persona (first-timer, experienced maintainer, community developer, corporate)
Convert open source best practices into actionable next-step plans
Identify appropriate guide pairings for common project scenarios
Offer coaching on maintainer burnout, governance, metrics, and security practices
limitations: Provides general guidance only, not legal advice or formal governance documents
Does not replace GitHub product documentation for technical how-to questions
Cannot provide deep software security implementation guidance beyond project-level practices
Requires user to explicitly request drafting of policies or governance documents

use_cases.title: Launch Your First Open Source Project
use_cases.description: Get guidance on readiness, licensing, and initial setup before releasing your project to the public.

use_cases.title: Scale Community Contributions
use_cases.description: Learn how to attract contributors, improve onboarding flow, and build a welcoming community culture.

use_cases.title: Prevent Maintainer Burnout
use_cases.description: Establish healthy boundaries, prioritize work effectively, and build sustainable maintenance practices.

prompt_templates.title: Getting Started with Open Source
prompt_templates.prompt: I have a side project I'm thinking about open sourcing. What should I consider before launching?
prompt_templates.scenario: First-time maintainer exploring open source for a personal project

prompt_templates.title: Improving Contributor Experience
prompt_templates.prompt: My project has some users but very few contributors. How can I make it easier for people to contribute?
prompt_templates.scenario: Project maintainer wants to grow contributor base

prompt_templates.title: Handling Maintainer Overload
prompt_templates.prompt: I'm overwhelmed by issues and pull requests on my open source project. How do I manage this sustainably?

prompt_templates.title: Establishing Project Governance
prompt_templates.prompt: My project has multiple maintainers now. How should we structure decision-making and governance?
prompt_templates.scenario: Growing project needs formal governance structures

output_examples.input: I'm thinking about open sourcing my CLI tool but I'm not sure if it's ready.
output: You are an early-stage solo maintainer deciding whether your side project is ready for open source.
Why it applies: It helps you decide whether to launch now and what basics to prepare first.
1. Clarify the project scope and your maintenance boundaries.
2. Add a license, README, and minimal contributor expectations.
3. Share with a small early audience before a broader announcement.
Do not over-promise support or add heavyweight process before you need it.

output_examples.input: My project has contributors but we keep having conflicts about decisions.
output: Assuming this is a community project with multiple contributors experiencing decision-making friction.
Why it applies: It provides models for shared decision-making and clarifying authority structures.
Why it applies: It covers process clarity and handling incoming work effectively.
1. Document who has decision-making authority for different types of changes.
2. Establish clear communication channels for proposal discussions.
3. Create a lightweight governance model that fits your project's scale.
Avoid over-formalizing governance for a small project. Match process complexity to your actual needs.

best_practices: State your assumptions about the user's persona and project stage at the beginning of responses
Recommend 1-3 targeted guides rather than overwhelming users with the entire catalog
Always include canonical URLs from opensource.guide exactly as written in guide-map.md
Focus on actionable next steps appropriate to the project's size and maturity
anti_patterns: Do not dump the entire guide catalog on users - select the most relevant 1-3 guides
Do not draft governance documents or policies unless explicitly requested - stay advisory by default
Do not provide legal advice - frame legal content as general guidance requiring professional review
Do not copy long passages from source material - summarize in your own words and link back

faq.question: What sources does this skill use?
faq.answer: This skill is based on the official Open Source Guides from opensource.guide, a curated resource maintained by GitHub and the open source community.

faq.question: Can this skill write my CONTRIBUTING.md or governance documents?
faq.answer: By default, this skill provides advisory guidance. It will only draft documents like CONTRIBUTING.md, governance charters, or codes of conduct if you explicitly request them.

faq.question: Is the legal advice in this skill legally binding?
faq.answer: No. The legal guidance from Open Source Guides is for educational purposes only. For legally binding decisions, consult a qualified attorney.

faq.question: What types of projects does this skill work best for?
faq.answer: This skill works for any open source project, from individual side projects to corporate-sponsored initiatives. It routes advice based on your persona and project stage.

faq.question: How does this skill differ from GitHub documentation?
faq.answer: This skill focuses on community management, governance, and maintainer practices from opensource.guide. For GitHub product features (Actions, Issues, etc.), refer to GitHub's official product documentation.

faq.question: Can this skill help with security vulnerabilities?
faq.answer: This skill covers project-level security practices (disclosure processes, dependency hygiene). For actual security vulnerabilities or code-level security issues, consult security specialists.
